# Purchase Order Validation ( POV )

This procedure is to be followed each time we have a new Purchase order or a maintenance renewal, and is managed by [regional Finance](https://docs.google.com/a/expertflow.com/document/d/1zjH__YjoUJHKGCfzzEfXA0Oi2xrQed6S72HkkFQhVDg/edit#) (Moses for East Africa, Arslan & Ihtesham for PK, Swiss, Maghreb, Nigeria). The goal is to send PO acceptance within at most five business days once we receive a PO.  
With the POV, responsibility for a lead passes from sales/ presales to the project team, and we fully commit to deliver to a certain scope and timeline. After POV, all meetings/ information gathering will be assumed by the project manager.  
At the end of the project or a maintenance contract that is not renewed, [a sign-off is done](https://docs.google.com/a/expertflow.com/document/d/1_VOH5ch_H_beSl9dOx9adr4Ko592KA2i3ucwwrr9jgU/edit#heading=h.slw4tnfqukos).

# **Identify POs and Offers**

Identify the final offer (OFFS \- Offer-Sales side), Purchase order from the client (POS \- Purchase order \- Salesside), Purchase order from Expertflow to Supplier (POB) and offers from the suppliers (OFFB)~~, SOW’s~~.  The presales and the sales need to give finance this information. Clarify the scope. If anything is unclear, involve presales.  
The goal is not yet to have a final LLD (Low level design)\! Detailed design is always part of the project, NOT part of the presales phase/ POV. The goal of PO Validation is to frame the project roughly in terms of commercial scope, timelines and pre-requirements, and to hand over formally to the project manager.  
Make sure all documents are available on the folder for the client (clients\\\<clientname\>). Put all old commercial documents in a folder “old commercial”, and add the same information on a Note in the relevant project on Hubspot.

## **Ordering to suppliers/ project finance**

Do NOT order to suppliers until the latest possible moment or until the sales director or the project manager asks to do that. Follow [these guidelines](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1TSHwhuwH2oDeS18ZkbsgOFR7ko3iULY22Psok88gks8/edit#heading=h.uj1rwayapld6) for ordering.  
Ensure that any renewable customer-wise is tracked on Hubspot under maintenance contract.  
Ensure that any renewable vendor-facing contract i[s tracked under the vendor link](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1SrjIE0p0zVB4fk8IpRUykytwiWjrdl_SWGorTCcjrp0/edit#heading=h.4qj705hqo9i8), and that a link from there to Hubspot is updated. 

## **EF Software Products \- Software Entitlement Certificate (Online deals)** During the POV, align the received PO with final offer and involve Pre-sales/Product team to verify and create a Software Entitlement Certificate for all EF Software products which are purchased by the partner/client. This should normally be sent along with the 1st Invoice.

Pre-sales/Product team can get the generic document by selecting the “Software Entitlement Certificate” from [ExpertFlow Template Gallery](https://docs.google.com/document/u/0/?tgif=d) and add the relevant products purchased by the partner/client.

The ultimate goal is to automate this procedure when a partner/client purchases ExpertFlow Software whether it is online deals or MEA deals.

## **Valid offer in Bidstruct**

Make sure that the offer in that has been ordered exists also in Bidstruct, and that the final amounts, as well as the suppliers exist as well\! If they don’t exist on Bidstruct, create these offers \- later on also adjust the rebates so that the final amounts per supplier are correct.  
If the suppliers don’t exist in an offer of that project, then you won’t be able to select a counterpart in the Invoice for a project. Only vendors that exist in an offer for the project will appear in the counterparts for an invoice. Bidstruct allows you intentionally only to select as counterparts the companies that were suppliers, clients or partners for that particular project. Also look at 00:36:18 of    
[~~https://expertflow.webex.com/expertflow/ldr.php?RCID=56ca28bb94ad7019b8ec65dab40b1764~~](https://expertflow.webex.com/expertflow/ldr.php?RCID=56ca28bb94ad7019b8ec65dab40b1764)  
[https://drive.google.com/a/expertflow.com/\#folders/0B3xH8IL9lozHLVZIMXQzSGdQaW8](https://drive.google.com/a/expertflow.com/#folders/0B3xH8IL9lozHLVZIMXQzSGdQaW8)  
\=\> I am about to upload these videos to youtube or vimeo for greater convenience.

### **Why have valid offers for a project**

We will soon have reports that will allow us to compare per supplier per project the sum that we ordered, and then compare this to the invoices that we paid to this particular supplier (or the amounts that we receive for a project from our clients). So for example if we order 50k$ from EFZ for a project, and we paid 30k$ already, we know that we still owe 20k$. This will help us ensure that the sum of all the invoices are always equivalent to the sum of all things bought or sold. It will also guarantee that we do POV properly as the reports will then automatically compare the sums.  
As a finance person, you can expect and ask presales/ sales to properly create ordered offers in Bidstruct, before you start POV (or at least give you all the required details, so that you can correct and adjust).

### **Multi-year or maintenance contracts: POC**

Create smaller internal invoices for [Percentage of Completion (POC)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percentage-of-completion_method) revenue demarcation for multi-year contracts.

# **Have a POV meeting with the presales, project managers and responsible delivery persons**

1. all content and lines of the offer, pos, offb's. Involve subject-matter experts (the engineer who will deploy at the end) to discuss each and every point, and make sure all questions are being raised.  
2. payment terms  
3. timelines  
4. any additional cost  
5. Plan of when we should release POB's and pay INVB's  
6. discuss traveling times / travel Expenses (Hotel , Taxi) with the partner.  
7. Is any development or customized reports planned or happening? Make sure that the head of development (or the reporting team in case of reporting) is informed of what the planned work is all about. There might be similar solutions already. For new developments on existing products, also involve the relevant product manager. Also, if there is any support during the development or afterwards required from the team in Pk, then dev needs to be involved.  
8. Is there any legal requirement from an outside (not in-country) location? If yes, inform that partner/customer\!  
9. Verify that all softwares that are to be used in that project are compatible and have been tested with each other \- if this is not documented, involve QA, and get their timeline for testing and approval.  
10. Create a new Project on Jira, so that all the team members can log their time spent on that particular project.  
11. Define how the customer is organized, who reports to whom and is responsible for what. Define who from the customer should comment on overall customer satisfaction to Expertflow ([see here](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1l51VyXBRgPMWB9FXI6XU7Zkr2G3TUSGBO9odMPLHxnY/) \- update on Hubspot), and inform the marketing team accordingly. For maintenance projects, define the frequency (quarterly, biannual, annual).

\=\> input and requirements will come from presales/ sales, and all requirement/ responsibilities are formally written down(Minutes of Meeting), added to a document in GDrive. Copy the same content a note under the relevant client project in Hubspot, including a link to the final offer/ order.

# **Prepare TimeTracking \- Tempo Budget**

* Create a [Jira Project, as well as Tempo Folio (= Budget)](http://project.expertflow.com:8080/secure/ManageFolio.jspa) for the project [in ECM mode](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mT7oYesaCrs&t=0s&index=3&list=PLgYwuDZsqFAcirObQNOIjt5J2pp-HlM7c). [Watch tutorials here](https://www.tempo.io/past-webinars) (the ones in dark blue).   
* For all professional services, create a time budget in number of hours, set hourly rates, and assign the project team. (Do this by estimating an effort for each task (=Jira Issue))  
* For all external costs (travel, suppliers,...), create fixed costs/ expenditures in Tempo Budget  
* Select a start and end date of the project.  
* The assigned hourly budget has to be agreed between the engineers/ project managers that will execute the project, and the sales. If no agreement can be found, the sales decides whether he wants to continue doing this project or not.  
* Group that Folio under the regional Portfolio, so that the regional director has a real-time view on which projects are at-cost or not.

Add deliverables for SW development [by updating this list](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1Nxdy9X7X2TRHL8fMM-B-PoUX3NRHQTbwYPxoihpX8kM).

# **Invoices \- Bidstruct**

1. Bidstruct: Split up the project to [what is main project, and what is maintenance](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1zjH__YjoUJHKGCfzzEfXA0Oi2xrQed6S72HkkFQhVDg/edit#heading=h.cpv3mt8isfey). Create a link in the comments field between the two.  
2. Bidstruct: mention final content, offers of all suppliers, calculate expected profit, set to status "Project" and 100% probability. Write the assigned project manager in the entry. Upload the POS to /internal/documents. Insert a link to the Purchase Order into the "Comment" field.  
3. Bidstruct: Prepare all INVS's (prepare order acceptance and send; prepare all other future invoices, so that the sum of the invoices \= sum of order) \- both for maintenance and main project. put as "date paid"/ “Payment date” the date when we expect to be paid. “Due Date” when it is actually due/ to which period/ year this belongs to and “Sent Date” when it is actually sent.  
4. Prepare Invoices for suppliers/vendors INVB's with timelines in [http://app.bidstruct.com///\#/invoice](http://app.bidstruct.com///#/invoice)  
5. ~~Create a new opportunity in "Project" for maintenance renewal, one year after expected NRFU, with a 60% probability, and the earnings being equal to the maintenance value.~~  
6. Create a new invoice in status "prepared" for maintenance renewal, to be sent three months before the expiry of the maintenance. Along with when which licenses expire.  
7. Write a Google Doc that summarizes who is responsible for what during the project.

# **CRM Update \- Hubspot**

* Update the project as won in Hubspot  
* For maintenance projects, create a new entry here (Deal Stage “MaintenanceRenewal” with the date being the expiry date of the contract, along with license expiry date: [https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/2944394/deals/list/view/5579819/](https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/2944394/deals/list/view/5579819/)  
  * Create an entry for all the customers and the recurring vendor contracts (maintenance contracts, security certificates,...) that will auto-renew, so that we can cancel or renew in time.  
* Add all products that are part of this project (also add CCX or CCE, if we’re doing the maintenance for these). This allows presales to rapidly find where we have active maintenance contracts for which products.  
* Link the partner company and customer company in the deal record

It is the responsibility of Finance to ensure that recurring contracts are being renewed or canceled in time by systematically reviewing every month all contracts that will expire/ need to be renewed.

# **Initial project plan (only if customer issue sharing required)**

To be initiated by the project manager if he wishes to share tasks (can also happen later during the project

* Verify that the assigned project manager creates an initial project plan (high-level deliverables, especially for tasks to be done by external parties: client, partner, other regions) in Jira  
* Create a new project on project.expertflow.com![][image1]   
* Create a new group of users with the customer domain name, that has access to this one project only, [see here how to do it](https://community.atlassian.com/t5/Jira-questions/How-to-give-access-to-just-one-project/qaq-p/297666)  
* Talk to a [JIRA User Administrator](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1MXVNAfpkWCrdvUBfcrhGddZTCO9t2gG3kkcgXcpcJ8g/edit#heading=h.21oo2i2zvsmw) if you don’t have rights to open a new project (Imran, Arslan, Asjad, Zaeem).   
  * Associate the Jira project to the right Tempo Account (= Region; or ProductDev, or general)  
  * Create initial tasks with the functional managers (DevOps, Reporting, cc)  
  * estimate efforts for each task or epic  
  * add tags (DevOps, Reporting, cc ), so that the relevant work will show up on the relevant Sprint  .  
  * Give Jira Core user access to 1-2 persons of the customers, so that task lists can be shared with them

# **Statement of work (SOW) and FAT/ NRFU test document**

Before you start any technical work, write a detailed SOW or LLD, along with a test/ NRFU document. This will avoid any issues later on during implementation and testing, and limit scope creep.

# **Initial Invoice/ PO acceptance**

With the initial Invoice (make sure you discuss what needs to be communicated to the client and partner at this stage\!\!):

*Dear abc,*  
*My name is xxx, I am responsible for accounting.*   
*Thank you for your purchase order. We have validated your order and we hereby confirm that we can execute this project. Mention anything that we can not do or caveats that the project team mentioned during the internal kickoff, delays, ..\!\! The target start date is xxx, the target closure/ go-live date is yyy, or x weeks from the moment where our pre-requirements are met (remote access, clarification on the SOW...). From your side, we expect abc, and.*  
*The person responsible for the project execution is qqq. In case you need any technical escalation, please involve zzz (Asad/ Asjad), and for commercial escalation (regional sales, or Asjad for delivery).*   
*Could you please indicate who are the counterparties for the project, accounting and commercials on your side as well?*  
*In terms of joint working hours, I think the hours where we would both be available is daily from xAM to yPM your time (GMT-xyz).*  
*Our payment terms are x% up-front, y% then, z% then.*  
*Please find attached our initial invoice with the details of our bank account.*

## **Other things to communicate (project manager or finance, in coordination with each other)**

* Working hours (usually we have timeshifts; suggest hours where both the partner/ client and Expertflow are likely going to work. Often these are only 1-2 hours per day, so these should be reserved up-front as well as regular follow-up meetings). Communicate only in the customer’s time zones, and suggest only normal business hours in the customer’s time. Don’t mention which time zones or country you work in, or state it broadly (“our offices are in European to South Asian Time zones”)  
* Product Limitations as listed on docs.expertflow.com in the current release must be shared/ discussed with the customer before finishing POV, for example [Release Notes \- Hybrid Chat (expertflow.com)](http://docs.expertflow.com/chat/latest/release-notes) or [Release Notes \- Voice Recording Solution (expertflow.com)](http://docs.expertflow.com/vrs/latest/release-notes) for Compatibility (Versions, CCX/ CCE), and Major/ Minor issues.  
* Caveats/ limitations/ timelines
